title: Garlic Herb Chicken Rice Bowl
description: Tender garlic herb chicken served over fluffy jasmine rice with roasted vegetables. A complete, satisfying meal.
instructions: Rinse rice and cook in chicken broth according to package directions. Keep warm.
Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, rosemary, and thyme.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 6-7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
In the same skillet, add remaining oil and sauté garlic for 30 seconds. Add broccoli and cook 4-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Divide rice among bowls, top with sliced chicken and broccoli. Drizzle with lemon juice and serve.

title: Classic Beef Fried Rice
description: Restaurant-style beef fried rice with tender strips of beef, scrambled eggs, and crisp vegetables. Better than takeout.
instructions: Season beef strips with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tbsp avocado oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry beef 2-3 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add 1 tbsp oil to wok. Scramble eggs until just set, break into pieces. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry carrots for 2 minutes, then add garlic and ginger for 30 seconds.
Add cold rice, breaking up clumps. Stir-fry 3-4 minutes until rice is heated and slightly crispy.
Add coconut aminos, peas, beef, and eggs. Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes.
Drizzle with sesame oil, top with green onions, and serve immediately.

title: Fragrant Lamb Biryani
description: An aromatic Indian rice dish with tender spiced lamb, caramelized onions, and fragrant basmati rice layered and slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: Marinate lamb in yogurt, ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, and 1 tsp salt for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Soak basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. Parboil rice with whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, bay leaves) until 70% cooked. Drain and set aside.
Heat ghee in a large Dutch oven. Fry onions until deep golden brown and crispy, about 20 minutes. Remove half for garnish.
Add marinated lamb to the pot with remaining onions. Cook over medium heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Layer parboiled rice over the lamb. Drizzle saffron milk over the rice. Sprinkle with cilantro and mint.
Cover tightly with foil and lid. Cook on lowest heat for 45 minutes (dum cooking).
Gently mix layers when serving. Garnish with reserved fried onions.

title: Salmon Teriyaki Rice Bowls
description: Glazed teriyaki salmon over steamed rice with fresh cucumber, avocado, and a drizzle of spicy mayo.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions. Keep warm.
Whisk together coconut aminos, honey, rice vinegar, ginger, and garlic for the teriyaki sauce.
Heat sesame oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Place salmon skin-side up and cook 4 minutes.
Flip salmon, pour half the teriyaki sauce over the fish. Cook 4-5 minutes, basting occasionally, until salmon is cooked through and glazed.
Divide rice among bowls. Top with salmon, avocado slices, and cucumber.
Drizzle with remaining teriyaki sauce, sprinkle with sesame seeds and green onions.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala with Rice
description: Tender chunks of marinated chicken in a creamy, spiced tomato sauce served over fragrant basmati rice.
instructions: Mix yogurt with 1 tbsp garam masala, cumin, paprika, turmeric, and half the garlic and ginger. Marinate chicken for at least 1 hour.
Cook basmati rice according to package directions.
Thread chicken onto skewers (or spread on a baking sheet). Broil 4-5 minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Heat ghee in a large pan. Sauté onion until golden, about 8 minutes. Add remaining garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes, remaining garam masala, cayenne, and salt. Simmer 15 minutes.
Stir in coconut cream and add cooked chicken. Simmer 5 more minutes.
Serve over basmati rice, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Mediterranean Lamb Rice Pilaf
description: Aromatic rice pilaf with seasoned ground lamb, toasted pine nuts, dried cranberries, and fresh herbs.
instructions: Toast pine nuts in a dry skillet until golden. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large pot. Brown ground lamb with cinnamon, allspice, cumin, salt, and pepper. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Add rice and stir to coat with oil. Toast for 2 minutes.
Pour in chicken broth, bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer 18 minutes.
Fluff rice with a fork. Fold in cooked lamb, pine nuts, dried cranberries, mint, parsley, and lemon zest.
Serve warm as a main dish or side.

title: One-Pot Lemon Herb Chicken and Rice
description: A comforting one-pot meal with tender chicken thighs, fluffy rice, and bright lemon-herb flavors.
instructions: Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, and paprika.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear chicken skin-side down for 5-6 minutes until golden. Flip and cook 2 more minutes. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Add rice and stir to toast for 1 minute.
Pour in chicken broth, lemon juice, half the lemon zest, and thyme sprigs. Stir well.
Nestle chicken thighs on top of rice, skin-side up. Bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook on low heat for 25-30 minutes until rice is tender and chicken reaches 165°F.
Garnish with remaining lemon zest, oregano, and parsley before serving.

title: Korean Beef Bulgogi Rice Bowls
description: Sweet and savory Korean marinated beef served over rice with pickled vegetables and a fried egg.
instructions: Mix coconut aminos, 2 tbsp sesame oil, honey, grated pear, garlic, ginger, and half the green onions for marinade. Marinate beef for at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Quick-pickle cucumbers: toss with rice vinegar and a pinch of salt. Refrigerate.
Cook rice according to package directions.
Heat a large skillet or grill pan over high heat. Cook beef in batches, 2-3 minutes per side, until caramelized.
Fry eggs sunny-side up in remaining sesame oil.
Divide rice among bowls. Top with bulgogi, pickled cucumber, and fried egg.
Garnish with remaining green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Classic Stuffed Bell Peppers
description: Colorful bell peppers stuffed with seasoned ground beef and rice, topped with tomato sauce.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Cut tops off peppers and remove seeds. Place in a baking dish.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Sauté onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Add ground beef, breaking it up. Cook until browned. Drain excess fat.
Stir in cooked rice, half the tomato sauce, Italian seasoning, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Stuff each pepper with the meat mixture, packing firmly.
Pour remaining tomato sauce over the stuffed peppers. Add 1/4 cup water to the bottom of the dish.
Cover with foil and bake 35 minutes. Remove foil and bake 10 more minutes.
Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.

title: Coconut Chicken Curry with Rice
description: Creamy Thai-inspired coconut curry with tender chicken, vegetables, and aromatic spices over jasmine rice.
instructions: Cook jasmine rice according to package directions.
Season chicken with curry powder and salt. Heat coconut oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Brown chicken on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté onion for 3 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Add bell peppers and zucchini. Cook 3 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and fish sauce. Bring to a simmer.
Return chicken to the pan. Simmer 15 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in lime juice and fresh basil.
Serve curry over jasmine rice.

title: Arroz con Pollo (Spanish Chicken and Rice)
description: Classic Spanish one-pot dish with saffron-infused rice, tender chicken, and colorful vegetables.
instructions: Bloom saffron in warm broth for 10 minutes.
Season chicken with salt, pepper, and 1 tsp paprika. Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven. Brown chicken on all sides, about 8 minutes total. Remove and set aside.
Add onion and bell peppers. Sauté 5 minutes. Add garlic, remaining paprika, and cumin. Cook 1 minute.
Add rice and stir to coat. Pour in saffron-infused broth and diced tomatoes.
Nestle chicken pieces on top. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer 25 minutes.
Add peas, cover, and cook 5 more minutes until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve family-style.

title: Ginger Beef with Broccoli and Rice
description: A lighter take on the Chinese takeout classic with fresh ginger, tender beef, and crisp broccoli.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions.
Mix coconut aminos, rice vinegar, and honey for sauce. Set aside.
Heat 2 tbsp avocado oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry beef in batches until browned, 2-3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry broccoli for 3 minutes. Add ginger and garlic, cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Return beef to wok. Pour in sauce. Add arrowroot slurry and toss until sauce thickens, about 1 minute.
Drizzle with sesame oil. Serve over rice topped with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Lamb Kofta with Saffron Rice
description: Spiced lamb kebabs served over aromatic saffron rice with cooling cucumber yogurt sauce.
instructions: Cook basmati rice with saffron water added to the cooking liquid.
Mix ground lamb with grated onion, garlic, parsley, half the mint, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cayenne, and 1 tsp salt. Form into oval-shaped koftas around skewers.
Make yogurt sauce: combine yogurt, cucumber, remaining mint, and a pinch of salt.
Brush koftas with olive oil. Grill or broil 4-5 minutes per side until cooked through with nice char marks.
Fluff saffron rice and divide among plates. Top with lamb koftas.
Serve with cucumber yogurt sauce on the side.

title: Hawaiian Chicken Rice Bowls
description: Sweet and tangy Hawaiian-style chicken with grilled pineapple, served over coconut rice.
instructions: Cook rice with coconut milk plus 1 cup water for coconut rice.
Mix coconut aminos, pineapple juice, honey, rice vinegar, garlic, and ginger for sauce.
Heat oil in a large skillet. Season chicken with salt and cook until golden, about 6 minutes. Remove.
Add bell pepper and pineapple chunks. Cook 3-4 minutes until slightly charred.
Return chicken to pan. Pour in sauce. Simmer 5 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce is thickened.
Serve over coconut rice, topped with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Creamy Mushroom Beef and Rice Casserole
description: A comforting baked casserole with seasoned ground beef, mushrooms, and rice in a creamy sauce.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet. Brown ground beef, breaking it up. Season with salt and pepper. Remove and drain.
In the same skillet, sauté mushrooms until golden, about 6 minutes. Add onion and cook 4 minutes. Add garlic and thyme, cook 1 minute.
Stir in uncooked rice. Add beef broth, coconut cream, and Dijon mustard. Mix well.
Return beef to the skillet. Stir to combine. Bring to a simmer.
Cover tightly with foil and bake 40-45 minutes until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Let rest 5 minutes. Fluff with fork, garnish with parsley, and serve.

title: Mediterranean Cod and Rice Traybake
description: Baked cod fillets over a bed of seasoned rice with tomatoes, olives, and fresh herbs - all in one pan.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a 9x13 baking dish, combine rice, broth, 2 tbsp olive oil, garlic, oregano, and 1 tsp salt. Stir well.
Scatter cherry tomatoes and olives over the rice.
Cover tightly with foil. Bake 25 minutes until rice is almost tender.
Season cod with remaining salt, pepper, and olive oil. Place fillets on top of rice. Add lemon slices and capers.
Bake uncovered 15-18 minutes until cod flakes easily.
Garnish with fresh dill and serve directly from the pan.

title: Turkey Rice Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
description: Tender turkey meatballs made with rice, simmered in a rich tomato herb sauce. Perfect over cauliflower mash or more rice.
instructions: Mix ground turkey with cooked rice, egg, grated onion, half the garlic, half the parsley, 1 tsp Italian seasoning, paprika, 1 tsp salt, and pepper. Form into 24 meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Brown meatballs on all sides, about 6 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, sauté diced onion for 4 minutes. Add remaining garlic, cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes, remaining Italian seasoning, and remaining salt. Simmer 10 minutes.
Return meatballs to the sauce. Cover and simmer 20 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook jasmine rice for serving.
Serve meatballs and sauce over rice, garnished with remaining parsley.

title: Cajun Chicken and Rice Skillet
description: Bold Cajun-spiced chicken with bell peppers, celery, and rice in one flavorful skillet meal.
instructions: Season chicken with Cajun seasoning, paprika, and cayenne.
Heat olive oil in a large deep skillet over medium-high heat. Brown chicken on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Add onion, bell peppers, and celery. Sauté 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Add rice and stir to coat. Pour in chicken broth and diced tomatoes with their juices. Add thyme and salt.
Return chicken to the skillet. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer 20-25 minutes until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through.
Fluff rice, taste and adjust seasonings. Garnish with green onions.

title: Chinese Beef and Tomato over Rice
description: A classic Chinese-American dish featuring tender beef and fresh tomatoes in a savory-sweet sauce.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions.
Mix coconut aminos, tomato paste, honey, rice vinegar, and 2 tbsp water for sauce.
Season beef with salt and white pepper. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry beef until just browned, about 2 minutes. Remove.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry onion for 2 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds.
Add tomato wedges. Stir-fry gently for 2 minutes.
Return beef to wok. Add sauce and bring to a simmer. Add arrowroot slurry and cook until sauce thickens.
Serve immediately over hot jasmine rice, topped with green onions.

title: Persian Herb Rice with Roasted Chicken
description: Fragrant Persian-style rice studded with fresh herbs, served alongside perfectly roasted chicken thighs.
instructions: Soak rice in salted water for 1 hour. Drain well.
Season chicken with turmeric, cumin, garlic, lemon juice, 1 tsp salt, and pepper. Marinate 30 minutes.
Roast chicken at 425°F for 35-40 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Meanwhile, parboil rice in salted boiling water until just barely tender, about 6 minutes. Drain.
Mix herbs together. In a heavy pot, melt ghee over medium heat. Layer rice and herbs alternately, ending with rice.
Drizzle saffron water over the top. Wrap lid in a towel and cover tightly. Cook on lowest heat for 35-40 minutes.
Gently mix rice with herbs (the bottom will form a crispy crust called tahdig). Serve with roasted chicken.

title: Chicken Vegetable Rice Soup
description: A hearty, healing soup packed with chicken, vegetables, and rice. Perfect for cold days or when you need comfort.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper, then brown on both sides. Remove and set aside.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Sauté 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Pour in chicken broth. Add bay leaves and thyme sprigs. Bring to a boil.
Return chicken to the pot. Reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken and shred. Discard bay leaves and thyme stems.
Add rice to the pot. Simmer 18-20 minutes until rice is tender.
Return shredded chicken to the soup. Stir in lemon juice and parsley.
Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve hot.

title: Greek Lemon Chicken with Herbed Rice
description: Bright, lemony Greek-style chicken with oregano, served over herb-flecked rice with olives and feta (optional).
instructions: Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper. Marinate chicken for at least 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Spread rice in a large roasting pan. Pour in chicken broth and half the marinade.
Arrange marinated chicken pieces on top of rice. Scatter lemon slices around.
Roast 40-45 minutes until chicken is golden and reaches 165°F, and rice has absorbed the liquid.
Scatter olives over the dish. Garnish with fresh dill.
Let rest 5 minutes before serving.

title: Black Pepper Beef with Jasmine Rice
description: A bold, peppery stir-fry with tender beef strips and crisp vegetables. Simple yet packed with flavor.
instructions: Cook jasmine rice according to package directions.
Toss beef slices with arrowroot powder and 1 tbsp black pepper.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry beef in batches until browned, 2 minutes. Remove.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry onion and bell peppers for 3 minutes. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds.
Return beef to wok. Add coconut aminos, fish sauce, and remaining black pepper.
Toss everything together for 1 minute until sauce coats the beef.
Serve immediately over jasmine rice, topped with green onions.
